name: Prioritize inbound requests with Zendesk and Microsoft Teams end to end

parameters.message: =✅ Prioritize inbound requests with Zendesk and Microsoft Teams end to en — run completed at {{ $now.toFormat('HH:mm') }}

parameters.content:
### Receive Inbound Webhook

Entry point: listens for POST calls on `/wf09994-ticket`. Use the TEST url while building, switch callers to the PRODUCTION url after publishing.

Next: Keep Valid Tickets

### Keep Valid Tickets

Drops items that fail the condition; only qualifying items continue (no false branch).

Next: Detect Sentiment

### Detect Sentiment

Scores the text's sentiment; ports fire positive / neutral / negative.

Ports: ①→ Escalate Negative — Microsoft Teams · ②→ Log Neutral Feedback, Capture Failure Context · ③→ Thank the Sender

⚠ failures exit via the error rail

### Google Gemini Chat Model

Chat model powering the AI step (model: `models/gemini-2.5-flash`). Swap providers here without rewiring.

Feeds **Detect Sentiment** as `languageModel`

🔑 Google AI API key

### Escalate Negative — Microsoft Teams

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Open Zendesk Ticket · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Open Zendesk Ticket

zendesk — `create` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Collect All Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Zendesk API token

### Log Neutral Feedback

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Collect All Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Thank the Sender

slack — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Collect All Outcomes · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Slack OAuth2

### Collect All Outcomes

Rejoins 3 branches into one stream (mode: append) so downstream logic runs once.

Next: Stamp Completion Time

### Stamp Completion Time

Stamps `completedAt` with the current time for auditing.

Next: Persist Run Metadata

### Persist Run Metadata

Saves key fields onto the execution record — searchable later in n8n's execution list.

Next: Load Previous Snapshot

### Load Previous Snapshot

googleSheets — `read` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Compute Drift vs Snapshot · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Compute Drift vs Snapshot

Custom JavaScript: Compare current metrics with the previous snapshot

Next: Fingerprint for Idempotency

### Fingerprint for Idempotency

Custom JavaScript: Build a stable idempotency key per ticket (djb2 hash, no deps)

Next: Drop Already-Processed Items

### Drop Already-Processed Items

Cross-execution dedupe: remembers keys it has processed before and drops repeats — the idempotency guard.

Next: Enrich with Reference Data

### Enrich with Reference Data

GET https://enrichment.example.com/v2/lookup — retries 3× with backoff on failure.

Ports: ①→ Fold In Enrichment Fields · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 API key / bearer for the target API

### Fold In Enrichment Fields

Shapes the item: writes `enrichedAt`, `enrichmentSource`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Summarize Run Stats

### Summarize Run Stats

Shapes the item: writes `runAt`, `itemCount`, `status`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

### Append Audit Row (Google Sheets)

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

Ports: ①→ Send Summary to Microsoft Teams · ②→ Capture Failure Context

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

### Send Summary to Microsoft Teams

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · ⚠ failures exit via the error rail · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

### Capture Failure Context

Shapes the item: writes `failedNode`, `errorMessage`, `workflow`. Keeps the schema explicit between stages.

Next: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

### Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

microsoftTeams — `chatMessage` operation against the connected account.

Next: Park in Dead-Letter Queue

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Microsoft OAuth2

nodes.name: Note: Escalate Failure to Microsoft Teams

parameters.content:
### Park in Dead-Letter Queue

googleSheets — `append` operation against the connected account.

↻ retries 3×/2s · 🔑 Google OAuth2 / service account

# Prioritize inbound requests with Zendesk and Microsoft Teams end to end

**Pattern:** Ticket Triage · **Level:** Production · **Trigger:** Webhook

## What it does
Prioritize inbound requests with Zendesk and Microsoft Teams end to end. It fires the moment an external system calls its webhook.

## Why it exists
Manual triage is slow and error-prone; this workflow makes the process repeatable, monitored and hands-off, with failures surfaced instead of silently lost.

## Main flow
Receive Inbound Webhook → Keep Valid Tickets → Detect Sentiment → Escalate Negative — Microsoft Teams → Open Zendesk Ticket → Collect All Outcomes → Stamp Completion Time → …

## Use it for
- Teams that want to ticket triage without manual effort
- Anyone automating triage in customer support
- Replacing a fragile manual process with a reliable workflow

## You get
- Standardizes the process every time
- Keeps your systems in sync
- Responds in seconds

## Before first run
Connect credentials: API key / bearer for the target API; Google AI API key; Google OAuth2 / service account; Microsoft OAuth2; Slack OAuth2; Zendesk API token.
Follow the color key: 🟦 entry · 🟪 AI · 🟫 actions · ⬜ utilities · 🟩 outputs · 🟥 error rail.
Each node has its own doc card underneath — read those before editing.

## 🛟 Error handling & retries

External calls retry **3× with 2s backoff**. Anything still failing exits through its node's error output into the red error rail → context is captured and the on-call channel is alerted. Failures are also parked in a dead-letter queue for replay. Set an n8n **Error Workflow** in settings as the final safety net.

## 🔐 Security

Never trust inbound payloads: the webhook path is unguessable. Add header auth or IP allow-listing for defense in depth. Secrets live in n8n credentials — never in node parameters.

## 🤖 AI cluster

Purple nodes form one AI unit: the root node orchestrates, sub-nodes plug in via typed ports (model / memory / tools / parser). Swap the chat model without rewiring anything else. Costs scale with tokens — keep prompts lean and cap iterations.

## 🛠 Customize me

Quick wins: point the data nodes at your own accounts (see 🔑 lines on each card), adjust the trigger cadence, and tune thresholds/labels to your triage process. Every node card explains its role — safe to extend at any marked branch.
